title: Package Boundaries, Interfaces, Value Ownership, and Lifecycle · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in directed owned Go crawler graph and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, audit, store, report, and command dependencies inward through consumer-owned interfaces.
Specify ownership and copy rules for URLs, headers, byte slices, DOM selections, records, errors, and report rows crossing boundaries.
Define minimal interfaces at use sites and retain concrete constructors and lifecycle ownership at composition boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Error Taxonomy
steps.instruction:
Order the Error Taxonomy Authorized site auditing and Go HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Error Taxonomy.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Error Taxonomy assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Error Taxonomy, keep Error Taxonomy connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 5113 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove directed packages, value ownership, interfaces, resources, errors, and lifecycle closure; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Wrap causal errors with operation and identity while preserving cancellation, timeout, policy rejection, retryability, and operator action.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Error Taxonomy · fault isolation · Debug Error Taxonomy, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Error Taxonomy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Error Taxonomy · fault isolation · Debug Error Taxonomy, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go team handles case 3303 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove directed packages, value ownership, interfaces, resources, errors, and lifecycle closure; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Wrap causal errors with operation and identity while preserving cancellation, timeout, policy rejection, retryability, and operator action.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Error Taxonomy · fault isolation · Debug Error Taxonomy, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Formatted error strings are enough for reliable program decisions.

stimulus.title: Error Taxonomy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Formatted error strings are enough for reliable program decisions.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Wrap causal errors with operation and identity while preserving cancellation, timeout, policy rejection, retryability, and operator action.

options.text: Another successful run of the same happy-path example. That claim does not survive the error taxonomy · fault isolation evidence boundary.

For error taxonomy · fault isolation, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.
A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Error Taxonomy.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Error Taxonomy
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-error-taxonomy-ro65b4" that uses case 4065 to prove directed packages, value ownership, interfaces, resources, errors, and lifecycle closure. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Error Taxonomy.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Error Taxonomy into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Error Taxonomy, keep Error Taxonomy connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Wrap causal errors with operation and identity while preserving cancellation, timeout, policy rejection, retryability, and operator action.
